one_line: An Indus Valley urban worker who maintains drains, soak-pits and bathing platforms in Mature Harappan cities like Mohenjo-daro and Harappa.

appearance.age_look: mid-20s, lean and wiry with light but visible muscle from daily manual labour
appearance.face: oval face with straight nose, medium-brown complexion, high cheekbones and gently arched brows; a few faint dust smudges and a tiny scar on one cheek; default expression focused and calm, with sharp, observant eyes that scan the surroundings.
appearance.hair: thick black hair pulled into a short, neat topknot at the back of the crown, sides kept tidy; a simple narrow headband of undyed cloth (off-white) tied around the forehead to keep sweat from dripping while working.
appearance.costume: a knee-length wrapped lower garment (dhoti-style loincloth) of coarse, undyed beige cotton, pleated in front and tied securely at the waist with a dark brown woven cord; over the torso a light, short-sleeved, hip-length cotton tunic in muted clay-ochre, loose enough for movement and fully covering the upper body; a simple rectangular shawl of fine cotton with a subtle narrow stripe pattern in indigo and white draped from the left shoulder across the chest and hanging behind the right hip, used for wiping hands and dust; ankles and lower calves wrapped with narrow linen bands for protection, leaving the feet in sturdy, flat leather sandals with thong straps; minimal jewellery, just a single plain copper bangle on the right wrist and a short, three-bead steatite necklace in dull white around the neck; overall textiles look handwoven with slightly irregular texture, no modern stitching, patterns limited to simple stripes along borders.
appearance.signature: a long, smooth wooden drain-rod with a bronze scraping blade at one end and a forked hook carved at the other, used to open, clean and probe covered street drains and soak-pits.

ref_desc: A lean mid-20s Harappan sanitation worker with medium-brown skin, black topknot and cloth headband, wearing beige dhoti, clay-ochre tunic, indigo-striped shawl, leather sandals, minimal copper and steatite jewellery, carrying a wooden drain-rod with bronze scraping blade in a beige, clay and indigo palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a Mature Harappan sanitation worker: a lean mid-20s man with medium-brown skin, oval face, high cheekbones and calm, focused expression, black hair in a short topknot and a narrow off-white cloth headband. He wears a knee-length beige wrapped dhoti-style lower garment tied with a dark brown cord, a simple hip-length clay-ochre cotton tunic fully covering the torso, and an indigo and white subtly striped rectangular shawl draped from left shoulder across his chest and hanging behind his right hip. His ankles are wrapped with narrow linen bands and he stands in flat dark brown leather sandals. Jewellery is minimal: one plain copper bangle on the right wrist and a short dull white steatite three-bead necklace. In his hands and at his side is his signature tool, a long smooth wooden drain-rod with a bronze scraping blade at one end and a forked hook at the other, clearly visible. Show front, side and three-quarter views in the same frame, keeping the beige, clay-ochre, indigo, dark brown and dull copper colour palette consistent, with historically plausible Indus Valley textiles and no modern details.
